description_long: This gatehouse features a robust stone construction with a prominent central archway, flanked by two thick rectangular pillars. Atop each pillar sits a classic lantern-style lamp, suggesting both decorative and functional lighting. The arch is inscribed with text, adding a formal or commemorative element to the structure. The entryway is secured by a pair of solid, dark metal gates, which are set within the arch and appear to be lockable. The stonework is weathered, with visible patches of moss or discoloration, indicating age and exposure to the elements. The overall design is symmetrical and utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation beyond the lamps and inscription.
description: A symmetrical stone gatehouse with a central arched entry, flanked by two pillars topped with lanterns and secured by metal gates.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched entryway, inscription, metal gates, and both lanterns clearly.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the stone pillars and the profile of one lantern.
description_view_2: Rear view of the gatehouse, displaying the arch and gates from behind, with both lanterns visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, showing the other pillar and lantern in profile.
attribute_entryway_design: Arched stone entryway with double metal gates and inscribed arch.
